concerns.text: Tasks T011b-T011d (Strategy B: Raw FASTQ processing) are listed as incomplete/unchecked, yet the Spec (FR-001) and Plan mandate a fallback mechanism if pre-processed data is unavailable. The task list fails to provide a concrete implementation path for this mandatory fallback, effectively weakening the requirement to 'ingest data' into 'ingest data IF available'.

The sample size validation logic (T015-C/D) is marked incomplete, creating a semantic gap where the pipeline may proceed with N < 50. The Spec (Edge Cases) and Plan (Critical Constraint) explicitly require halting execution if N < 50. A task list that does not enforce this halt violates the 'Sample-Size-Aware Validation' constitution principle.

Tasks T020a (CLR), T020b (Sensitivity), and T020c (Shannon) are unchecked. The Spec (FR-002, FR-003) mandates CLR transformation and Shannon diversity calculation as core deliverables. Leaving these as unimplemented tasks violates the functional requirements for User Story 2.

Responder definition tasks (T030a-d) are unchecked. The Spec (Assumptions) requires defining responders via seroconversion (≥4-fold) or absolute titer (≥40). Without these tasks, the predictive modeling (FR-006) cannot define the target variable, violating the core requirement to predict 'high/low responder status'.

Task T013b (LOD Sensitivity Analysis) is unchecked. The Spec (Edge Cases) explicitly requires handling subjects with titers below the limit of detection (imputation or exclusion) and documenting the choice. The absence of this task leaves a critical edge case unaddressed, violating the data hygiene and robustness requirements.

Task T034b (Threshold Sweep) is unchecked. The Spec (Assumptions) mandates a sensitivity analysis sweeping the responder threshold at ±10%. Omitting this task violates the requirement to verify model robustness to threshold definition.
